steps.title: Animal acclimation Gently place mouse on...
steps.instruction: Animal acclimation Gently place mouse on the stationary belt with the enclosure door closed. Allow 1–2 min for the animal to explore and calm before initiating belt movement.

steps.title: Initiate belt movement Start the treadmill...
steps.instruction: Initiate belt movement Start the treadmill at 10 cm/s, then increasing to 15 cm/s once the animal is walking. Allow the mouse to reach steady-state locomotion (consistent running without stumbling or stopping). This typically takes 10–30 s.

steps.title: Record gait Once steady-state locomotion is...
steps.instruction: Record gait Once steady-state locomotion is achieved, begin video capture for at least 5 full stride cycles (aim for 5–10 s of continuous recording). The ventral camera records paw placement for all four limbs simultaneously.

steps.title: Return mouse to home cage Stop...
steps.instruction: Return mouse to home cage Stop belt, open enclosure, and return mouse to its home cage. Clean belt surface with 70% ethanol before next animal.

steps.title: Automated analysis Load video into DigiGait Analyzer
steps.instruction: Automated analysis Load video into DigiGait Analyzer. The software automatically segments individual paw areas across frames and computes gait parameters including: stride length, stride frequency, stance width (front/hind), paw area, swing time, stance time, and symmetry indices.

steps.title: Data export and statistics Export gait parameters per animal
steps.instruction: Data export and statistics Export gait parameters per animal. Compare WT vs KO groups using appropriate tests.
